research_goal: Understand why internal teams aren't adopting Pulse, our internal analytics platform.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Thanks for making time, Dana. To start, how often do you actually use Pulse in a given week?

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: Honestly? Almost never anymore. I opened it maybe twice last quarter. I want to like it, because in theory everything I need is in there, but it just doesn't fit how I work day to day.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: What happens when you do open it? Walk me through a recent attempt.

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: So last month my VP asked for total committed pipeline for the West region. Should be a thirty-second thing. But in Pulse it's too complex to get a simple pipeline number out of it. I couldn't figure out the query builder at all, all these joins and filters and dimension dropdowns. I just need one number.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: When you couldn't get the query builder to cooperate, what did you do instead?

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: What I always do. I pulled the raw opportunity export and so I just export it to a spreadsheet and do it there. Took me five minutes with a pivot table. My whole team basically lives in that shared sheet now, it's our real source of truth, not Pulse.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: Setting the query builder aside, how does Pulse feel performance-wise when you run something real?

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: That's the other killer. It's slow, it just spins when I run anything real. A small test query is fine, but the second I scope a full quarter across all reps it sits there with the little spinner forever. I've literally walked to get coffee and come back and it's still loading.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: How does that compare to the spreadsheet workflow time-wise?

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: It's not even close. The export plus pivot is faster than waiting on one Pulse query to load. When I'm in back-to-back deal reviews I can't afford to babysit a spinner, I need the number now. So the math just always lands on the spreadsheet.

turns.speaker: agent
turns.text: If you could change one thing about Pulse, what would it be?

turns.speaker: interviewee
turns.text: Make getting a basic pipeline number dead simple and fast, that's the whole game. Oh, and a small thing, I wish it had a mobile app so I could glance at pipeline from my phone between meetings. But really, fix the simple-number path and you'd get me back.

summary: Dana, a Sales Ops Lead, avoids Pulse because its query builder is too complex for simple pipeline numbers and it's painfully slow on real queries, so she defaults to exporting raw data into a shared spreadsheet.
